channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Scientists Burn Homes to Figure out How to Best Protect Them in Wildfires
item.description: Add moving vegetation, mulch, wood fences and hot tubs with their highly flammable insultation several feet away and experts said you can protect houses from the increasing danger of wildfires on a warming planet. They have set fire to 13 houses because scientists need to burn to learn. It took less than three minutes for wind-whipped flames to go from licking the side of the house to shattering a window and working under the eaves to burn everything inside.

item.title: Damage Still Being Assessed After Wisconsin Storms, Tornadoes
item.description: The strong storms that stranded cars and caused structural damage to at least 75 homes in Wisconsin last Friday were caused by several tornadoes, according to a preliminary report by the National Weather Service. The NWS confirmed several tornadoes resulted in structural damage. The severe weather was triggered by a surface low-pressure system and cold front across the upper Midwest which drew unusually high moisture into the region, with dew points climbing into the lower 60s into central Wisconsin, the weather service said.

item.title: River Deltas Sinking Faster Than Sea Level Rise Pose Risk to Millions: Study
item.description: Many of the world’s largest river deltas are subsiding more quickly than global sea levels are rising, putting hundreds of millions of people at potential risk, according to a new study published in Nature . In 18 of the 40 deltas, this downward movement, known as subsidence, already exceeds local sea-level rise. The research offers the first detailed, high-resolution analysis of elevation loss across 40 river deltas around the world.

item.title: Lawmakers Gather to Talk About AI; Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Follow
item.description: A congressional subcommittee on April 16 held a roundtable discussion on the potential of artificial intelligence, which took a turn toward the existential as each lawmaker aired their anxieties about the rapidly evolving technology. Yassamin Ansari, D-Ariz., raised concerns about the Trump administration’s use of AI in the war with Iran, the technology’s intensive energy usage and its potential effects on the climate. While members of Congress elsewhere debated other major topics — the scope of the federal government’s surveillance powers, the war with Iran and funding the Department of Homeland Security — the House Oversight Committee’s subcommittee roundtable on “Artificial Intelligence and American Power” brought executives of AI firms, academics and those implementing AI at major companies together with lawmakers.

item.title: Getting firms fit to run
item.description: Getting authorised is more like a marathon than it might seem. Speech by Sheree Howard at the APCC Spring Conference 2026. Speaker: Sheree Howard, executive director of Authorisations Event: APCC Spring Conference 2026, London Delivered: 22 April 2026 Note: This is the speech as drafted which may differ from the delivered version Reading time: 8 minutes This weekend, tens of thousands of runners will line up in Greenwich Park for the start of the London Marathon.

item.title: PERILS launches 2026 IED, showing $150tn in global nat cat exposures
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has released the 2026 edition of its Industry Exposure Database (IED), providing USD $150 trillion of markets sums insured exposed to natural perils for 21 different countries, with like-for-like exposures also rising 4.7% year-on-year at constant exchange rates. In total the PERILS IED 2026 contains 67 individual market portfolios. The IED contains markets sums insured exposed to natural perils for 21 countries, including Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, France, Germany, Ireland, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, the Philippines, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Turkey, and the United Kingdom.

item.title: Supply-demand softening property at a pace “I’ll only describe as dumb” – Chubb CEO Greenberg
item.description: Speaking during the Chubb first-quarter earnings call today, CEO Evan Greenberg called out supply-demand factors related to capital in the insurance and reinsurance industry, as well as high levels of intermediation cost, as softening the property insurance market at a pace he called “dumb”. “In a number of important markets, property and financial lines pricing conditions are soft, with property pricing in those markets softening at a pace that, frankly, I’ll only describe as dumb,” he stated. On large account property risks, both admitted and excess and surplus lines (E&S), Greenberg said the pricing levels are judged to be inadequate, driving the pull-back by Chubb in Q1.

item.title: Evolving flood and drought patterns are reshaping global re/insurance risk: Aon
item.description: Broker Aon states that intensifying flood and drought patterns are reshaping risk profiles across global insurance and reinsurance markets, according to its 2026 Climate and Catastrophe Insight (CCI) report. Based on Aon’s Climate Risk Monitor, rainfall-driven (pluvial) flood risk in the United States could increase by roughly 12% under a medium-emissions scenario and around 19% under a high-emissions scenario by mid-century. Citing National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) data, Aon noted that only 2.6% of residential properties in US counties received NFIP payouts for 2025 flood events that were covered by NFIP policies.

item.title: Aon reports increasing climate risk from flood and drought in 2026 CCI findings
item.description: Aon plc, a global professional services firm, states that evolving flood and drought patterns are reshaping risk profiles across global insurance and reinsurance markets, according to its 2026 Climate and Catastrophe Insight (CCI) report released ahead of Earth Day. Based on Aon’s Climate Risk Monitor, rainfall-driven (pluvial) flood risk in the US could increase by roughly 12 percent under a medium-emissions scenario and about 19 percent under a high-emissions scenario by mid-century. Drawing on its catastrophe data and forward-looking climate modelling, Aon reports that physical climate risks are continuing to change, with implications for communities, policymakers and insurers.

item.title: Gallagher Re appoints Christopher Lynch as VP, US Casualty Fac Broker
item.description: Global reinsurance broker Gallagher Re has announced the appointment of Christopher Lynch as Vice President – US Casualty Facultative Broker. Lynch’s appointment also comes alongside a number of international facultative broking hires, including Odd Kåre Engh Mistereggen as Facultative Broker, EMEA ; Fabienne Marsoner as Broker, APAC Casualty FAC ; and Michael Stankard as Broker for UK Casualty Facultative. In a recent LinkedIn post, Gallagher Re said, “His experience spans a number of diverse casualty lines and a broad spectrum of markets, enabling him to provide thoughtful, data-driven insights and solutions.” This year, Gallagher Re has made a series of facultative hires, including the appointment of Kevin Ingram as Vice President – USA Property Facultative.

item.title: Arctic maritimes routes see growing interest, though commercial potential remains limited: Coface
item.description: With the conflict in the Middle East and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting global trade , a new study from Coface has highlighted that Arctic routes are attracting growing interest as potential alternatives. However, the firm has suggested that the commercial potential of Arctic routes will “remain limited” in the short term despite changes in navigation conditions due to climate change. “Operational constraints, the limited size of vessels and the specific costs of Arctic navigation prevent it, at this stage, from competing with the economies of scale of traditional routes,” Coface observed.

item.title: People Moves: Pekin Insurance Names Colvert as VP – Sales & Marketing
item.description: Pekin Insurance announced that John Colvert has joined the organization as Vice President – Sales & Marketing. In his new role, Colvert will lead Pekin Insurance’s Sales and Marketing teams and will report to Joel Jackson, Senior Vice President (SVP) and Chief Operating Officer – Property & Casualty; SVP – Life. Earlier in his career, Colvert held senior leadership roles at Nationwide Insurance, focusing on business development, sales operations, and underwriting.

item.title: Texas AG Sues Kratom Retailers for Deceptive Marketing
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a lawsuit against two California-based online kratom retailers for deceptively marketing and selling synthetic kratom products and adulterated products that are up to 96% 7-hydroxymitragynine (“7-OH”). The lawsuit is a part of an initiative by Attorney General Paxton to limit unlawful distribution of adulterated and contaminated kratom products across Texas. To protect consumers from the dangers of synthetic alkaloids and adulterated kratom products containing dangerous levels of 7-OH, the Texas Legislature enacted the Texas Kratom Consumer Health and Safety Protection Act in 2023.

item.title: World Insurance Buys Long Island Agency
item.description: Baldon, which was established in 2000, is a Long Island independent agency with a focus on New York’s commercial insurance market for hospitality operators, real estate investors and construction businesses. World Insurance Associates LLC reports that it acquired the business of Baldon Insurance Group of Patchogue, New York. “We are happy to be joining World, a company that has a wide variety of products and services we can now offer to our clients,” stated Tom Donahue, owner of Baldon Insurance Group.

item.title: Clear Group expands Scottish footprint with Spence Insurance acquisition
item.description: Clear Group has expanded its presence in Scotland following the acquisition of Spence Insurance, as it continues to grow its regional brokerage platform. The deal brings the Bathgate-based commercial insurance specialist into Clear Group’s network, strengthening its capabilities in the Scottish market while maintaining Spence Insurance’s local presence and client relationships. The acquisition aligns with Clear Group’s strategy of partnering with established, high-quality brokers to expand its footprint while preserving the personalised service that local clients value.

item.title: Telematics Drives Shift in Commercial Insurance
item.description: Commercial insurance is evolving from reactive risk transfer to continuous prevention through real-time telematics and behavioral data. In commercial auto insurance, for instance, telematics systems capture driving patterns such as acceleration, braking behavior, route selection, and exposure to high-risk environments. For decades, commercial insurance has operated on a largely reactive model.

item.title: Why Insurance Is Lagging on AI
item.description: The insurance sector has a well-documented mismatch between its AI ambition and operational readiness. While 82% of insurance companies believe AI will define the industry's future, only 14% have fully integrated it into their financial operations, and 52% describe their data governance frameworks as early-stage or still developing. All data cited in this article is from AutoRek's 2026 Insurance Operations and Financial Transformation Report, based on 250 interviews with insurance and healthcare insurance managers across the U.S.

item.title: The MGA Strategy for Scaling Your Book Without Doubling Your Headcount
item.description: Most MGAs obsess over loss ratios and technical pricing, but no matter how well you run a disciplined book, your margins can still dissipate, leaving little trace of the leak. When payments arrive as blind cash without the policy data attached, you end up subsidizing your own back-office overhead with your commissions. When you send a lump sum without the policy details attached, you’re essentially offloading your manual labor onto the Carrier’s home office, leaving them to guess which dollar belongs to which customer .

item.title: Mphasis acquires Theory+Practice
item.description: Mphasis has acquired Vancouver-based Theory+Practice Business Intelligence, the company behind Continuum AI, in a deal valued at up to CAD 30 million, including CAD 10 million upfront and as much as CAD 20 million in milestone-based contingent consideration. Founded in 2018, Theory+Practice developed Continuum AI as a platform that combines AI and behavioral economics to improve decision-making and buyer understanding. The acquisition adds a decision intelligence platform focused on areas such as pricing, demand forecasting, marketing, and supply chain decisions, and gives Mphasis more depth in retail and consumer packaged goods.

item.title: Canopy Connect Update Shows Clients Every Way to Share Insurance Info
item.description: “Not every client wants to share their insurance information the same way,” said Tolga Tezel , founder and CEO of Canopy Connect. Canopy Connect describes the update as a direct response to customer feedback from agencies that observed clients hesitating or dropping off at the first step of the intake process. Canopy Connect , the insurance data intake platform used by more than 20,000 insurance agents, has added a new sharing path page to Link Flows, a feature of its insurance data intake platform.

item.title: ERSM Grupo integra a la correduría Manuel Mora
item.description: ERSM Grupo y Correduría Manuel Mora, con sede en Santander y Madrid, han alcanzado un acuerdo por el que primero adquiere una participación inicial del 40% en la correduría , con opción de incrementarla en el futuro. Ambas partes subrayan que la integración respetará la identidad y los valores de Manuel Mora. Fundada hace más de 30 años en Santander , la correduría presta servicio a unos 3.000 clientes y alcanzó un volumen de primas intermediadas de 5 millones de euros en el último año.

item.title: How Leaders Can Maintain Humanity in the Modern Insurance Era
item.description: As artificial intelligence and automation become faster and more precise, insurance industry leaders are finding that the differentiator isn’t just better technology but how the people behind the technology are using it. “It’s an art,” said Gaurav Vasisht, executive director and CEO of The New York State Insurance Fund. “We think that’s a big part of how you build specialty insurance and reinsurance companies, putting those two things together.

item.title: Asia insurance rates fall for ninth straight quarter: Marsh
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Pacific insurance rates drop 12% in Q1, extending soft market streak to 2 years: Marsh
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA leads first crackdown on illegal crypto trading
item.description: The FCA has carried out its first operation with partners to disrupt illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading across multiple London locations. Working with HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit (SWROCU), the FCA targeted 8 premises suspected of illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading. Peer-to-peer trading is when individuals buy and sell crypto directly with each other, rather than using a centralised exchange and requires appropriate registration.

item.title: FCA and PRA confirm changes to streamline senior manager accountability and boost growth
item.description: The changes, which come as the first phase of a multi-stage package of reform from the Government and regulators, will maintain the core principle of senior leader accountability, and will benefit firms by: The Government’s further changes to the regime, published in its consultation response on 22 April, follow its consultation in 2025. By working with regulators to streamline the Senior Managers and Certification Regime, we are cutting unnecessary complexity, halving the administrative burden, and building a simpler, faster and more competitive system.' Sarah Pritchard, deputy chief executive at the FCA, said: ‘These joint reforms will keep consumers and markets protected while making the regime more proportionate. We’ve also used our current powers to streamline the regime now, so firms can benefit before future legislation unlocks even more efficiencies.' David Bailey, executive director for prudential policy at the PRA, said: ‘The SM&CR plays an important role in ensuring accountability in the provision of financial services, but it is right that we work to ensure it is well-targeted and efficient.

item.title: What The Record Venture Funding Quarter Actually Means For Your Startup’s Fundraise
item.description: Crunchbase just reported that $300 billion flowed into startups in Q1 2026 , the biggest quarter in venture history. Early-stage funding was up 41% year over year. That divergence matters for founders deciding what to build.

item.title: A Better Way To Fail: How This Platform Aims To Turn Startup Shutdowns Into Something Salvageable
item.description: Crunchbase News spoke with Yona via email about the new product, the rise in startup shutdowns, and how attitudes around failure are beginning to shift. Yona: We’ve seen strong year-over-year growth in startup shutdown activity over the past few years, and that trend has continued into 2026 so far. More than 90% of startups fail, but what happens after a company shuts down is far less understood, and often far more painful than it needs to be.

item.title: Australia’s 2025 extreme weather insured losses climb to AUD $4.8bn: ICA
item.description: New data released by the Insurance Council of Australia (ICA) shows that the total cost of extreme weather events that impacted Australia throughout 2025 generated AUD $4.8 billion in insured losses in 2025, up 727% on the previous year, as claim numbers spiked to 294,000, of which over $4.1 billion came from Queensland alone. The ICA noted that the total economic cost of extreme weather events in 2025, including insured losses and broader economic costs, is estimated to be over $8.6 billion. The ICA further highlighted that insured losses from extreme weather events stood at $2.35 billion in 2023 and $585 million in 2024, which clearly underscores how difficult these costs are to forecast from one year to the next.

item.title: Twelve Securis “cautious, but optimistic” about El Niño’s influence on hurricane season risk
item.description: Twelve Securis, the specialist catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment manager, has explained that it is “cautious, but optimistic about El Niño’s positive influence on risk” for the coming Atlantic hurricane season. In a commentary in advance of the hurricane season and in the wake of early forecasts for levels of storm activity in the Atlantic, Twelve Securis stated that, “there is increasing confidence in a strong El Niño. Hurricane Andrew was one example of a major storm that drove meaningful damage and insured losses during an El Niño, but Twelve Securis explains that hurricane Betsy is perhaps a better cautionary example.

item.title: Acrisure Re integrates Capital and Captives division to form Alternative Risk Solutions platform
item.description: Acrisure Re, the reinsurance and insurance division of global fintech Acrisure, has integrated its Capital and Captives division to create an end-to-end platform, as the company seeks to meet growing demand for alternative risk solutions. The division will help further strengthen Acrisure Re’s market offering across three connected pillars: Reinsurance (including programs), Corporate Advisory through Acrisure Re Corporate Advisory and Solutions (ARCAS), and Capital and Captives. Acrisure Re Capital and Captives also includes an established segregated cell capability, which Acrisure Re states allows for flexible and capital-efficient structures across both Life and Health and Property and Casualty lines, supporting solutions tailored to client risk profiles and business objectives.

item.title: Florida market DPW rises 1.3% at year end 2025: Gallagher Re
item.description: The overall Florida insurance market reported a 1.3% year-on-year increase in direct premiums written (DPW) to $33.3 billion at year-end 2025, according to Gallagher Re’s Florida Market Watch Report. The report showed that Florida Specialists (excluding ANTSP) saw DPW increase by 13.5% to $22.2 billion compared to year-end 2024, with growth largely driven by PURE and Slide. Gallagher Re noted that 46 of the 61 tracked companies recorded an underwriting gain in 2025, up from 39 in 2024.

item.title: Ransomware is shifting towards infrastructure-led exploitation, At-Bay reports
item.description: At-Bay, a US-based InsurSec provider offering cyber insurance and risk management services, reports in its 2026 InsurSec Report that ransomware activity is increasingly shaped by the exploitation of core infrastructure. At-Bay also reports that SonicWall appliances were the most frequently targeted VPN technology for the first time, appearing in 27% of ransomware-related claims it analysed. According to At-Bay’s analysis of more than 6,500 claims and over 100,000 policy years, 73% of ransomware incidents in 2025 began with VPN compromise, a figure At-Bay notes has nearly doubled within two years.

item.title: W. R. Berkley reports GWP of $3.8bn for Q1’26 as net income expands
item.description: Berkley Corporation has reported a rise in gross written premiums (GWP) for Q1’26 to $3.8 billion, compared with $3.7 billion in Q1’25. For the first quarter of 2026, the insurer reported net income of $515.2 million and a combined ratio of 90.7%, with catastrophe losses contributing 2.4 loss ratio points. Net income and operating income increased to $515.2 million and a record $514.3 million, respectively, while net investment income grew by 12.2% to a record $404.3 million.

item.title: Oil Trader CFOs Say Hormuz Closure Driving Wave of Disputes
item.description: “We expect a lot of financial disputes and a lot of force majeure,” Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. In normal times, Hormuz handles about a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas but it was effectively closed to non-Iranian shipping soon after the war started. Photograph: Cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz in February 2026; photo credit: Giuseppe Cacace/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: SoundHound AI to acquire LivePerson in $250m deal to expand conversational AI platform
item.description: The post SoundHound AI to acquire LivePerson in $250m deal to expand conversational AI platform appeared first on Insurtech Insights . SoundHound AI has agreed to acquire LivePerson in a transaction valuing the business at approximately $250 million, bringing together voice AI and digital messaging capabilities into a unified conversational platform. The deal includes an equity value of $43 million, representing a 22% premium, and provides SoundHound with access to LivePerson’s global enterprise client base and its platform, which manages around one billion messages each month.

item.title: Fuse Launches Watch: The Live Peril Dashboard for Commercial Insurance
item.description: Watch plots every active US peril on a single live map, forecasts severe-weather risk three days forward, and overlays the commodity and macro markets shaping insurance pricing — an independent, continuously updating view of the perils moving the commercial insurance market. NEW YORK, NY, April 21, 2026 — Fuse International, the AI-powered commercial insurance intelligence platform, today announced the general availability of Watch , a new product purpose-built for commercial insurance brokers and underwriters. Watch is organized around four views: a Peril Map plotting active events across the continental United States, a Live Feed of insurance-relevant alerts with line-of-business context, a Forecast covering the next three days of severe-weather risk, and Market Pulse , a live read on the macro and commodity signals driving insurance pricing.

item.title: Clear Group acquires Spence Insurance
item.description: The Clear Group acquired Spence Insurance , an independent insurance broker based in Scotland’s Central Belt. “Spence Insurance is a highly respected broker with strong local relationships and a clear focus on commercial clients. This partnership allows us to continue delivering the personal service our clients value, while benefiting from the additional resources and wider capabilities that Clear provides.” – Stewart Spence, Managing Director of Spence Insurance.

item.title: Tigerless Health to go public via $280M SPAC deal
item.description: New York-based Tigerless Health has agreed to go public through a merger with Piermont Valley Acquisition Corp. Founded in 2018, Tigerless Health is focused on using data and AI to simplify how consumers access and navigate insurance. “We founded Tigerless Health because we believed that navigating insurance should not require expertise, connections, or luck – it should be simple, transparent, and accessible to everyone.

item.title: DKV más que triplica su resultado neto de 2025 tras la salida de Muface
item.description: Han sido, según presentó ayer el director general financiero, Javier Cubría, los mejores resultados en la historia de la compañía, en un año como 2025 que fue “extraordinario” por la salida del concierto de Muface. En relación a Muface, el directivo confirmó que se han interpuesto sendos recursos ante juzgados de lo Contencioso-Administrativ o en los que la entidad reclama una compensación por las pérdidas que, sostiene, se produjeron por la insuficiencia de las primas fijadas en conjunto del concierto 2022-2024, por un lado, y en la prórroga forzosa que el Gobierno impuso durante los cinco primeros meses de 2025. DKV Seguros logró el pasado año un beneficio neto atribuido de 54,6 millones de euros, cifra que supone más que triplicar los 14,7 millones del ejercicio precedente.

item.title: Así estamos repensando la protección empresarial
item.description: Por eso, en Generali hemos dado un paso que para mí es especialmente importante: hemos creado una unidad nueva dirección comercial especializada en Empresas , un equipo que vive el terreno, que entiende las particularidades de cada actividad y que trabaja con una convicción muy sencilla: la protección tiene que ser útil, cercana y humana. Hoy ofrecemos una nueva propuesta de valor para todo el proceso, con una suscripción más ágil y con una propuesta de productos modular, flexible y fácil de entender , que permite que cada negocio configure su protección a medida, sin comprar más de lo que necesita ni quedarse corto en lo esencial. En definitiva, nuestra aspiración y nuestro compromiso no es otro que ser una compañía cercana y referente en el aseguramiento del tejido empresarial Español, en ello vamos a trabajar todo el equipo en todo el territorio.

item.title: Accede a las cifras de 110 aseguradoras españolas
item.description: INESE Data publica ‘Las aseguradoras españolas en cifras 2025’ . Este estudio permite acceder a las cuentas técnicas por ramos, balances, cuentas no técnicas, fondos propios y diversos ratios de siniestralidad, gastos y resultados de 2025 de 110 aseguradoras españolas , integradas en 67 grupos, con una cuota de mercado del 95,63%. El estudio permite conocer cómo han evolucionado las aseguradoras en estos parámetros, ya que los compara con la información anual definitiva presentada a la DGSFP correspondiente a 2024.

item.title: Adjusters: Why the Indemnification Clause Should Stay Top of Mind
item.description: It materializes months later, when the claims professional finally evaluates the clause and finds the carrier absorbing defense costs it was never obligated to pay or honoring indemnification demands that were never enforceable. On files with that level of exposure, the indemnification clause is not a background document. Treat the indemnification clause as an open item from the moment it appears in the file until it has been fully evaluated.

item.title: Purdue’s $5.5B Sentencing for Opioid Charges Delayed After Victims Show at Court
item.description: Related: Purdue Pharma Gets Court Nod for Bankruptcy Exit, Sackler Deal Arleo initially scheduled the hearing to occur on Zoom, but changed course during the proceedings and delayed it until April 28 after protesters and members of the public showed up at court to participate in the sentencing hearing. A federal judge on Tuesday delayed the planned criminal sentencing of Purdue Pharma by one week, saying she wanted to allow more public participation before concluding a criminal case over the company’s marketing of addictive opioid drugs. District Judge Madeline Cox Arleo was expected to accept Purdue’s 2020 guilty plea at a court hearing in Newark, New Jersey, which would have imposed a $3.5 billion criminal fine and $2 billion in criminal forfeiture against the company for its role in fueling an opioid epidemic that has claimed more than 1 million lives in the U.S.

item.title: Microsoft Must Face $2.8B UK Lawsuit Over Cloud Computing Licenses
item.description: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday. are separately examining the practices of Microsoft and other firms in cloud computing. They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because Microsoft charges higher wholesale prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper than Amazon’s AWS or Google Cloud.

item.title: General average declared after Singapore containership fire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Japan on high alert for second earthquake after first brings tsunamis, minor damage, injuries
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Newline promotes Rebecca Smith to senior underwriter for Australia PI team
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints Subhash Khanna as Director, Life Solutions, Reinsurance
item.description: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions, the reinsurance division of the global broking group, has appointed Subhash Khanna as a Director of the Life Solutions team within its Strategy and Technology Group, based in London. In the past, Khanna also served as a Senior Risk Analyst for Risk Management at McKinsey & Company, and as an Assistant Vice President at Swiss Re. Most recently, he served at Milliman for over 11 years, as Principal & Consulting Actuary and Consulting Actuary, based in both London and India.

item.title: Cowbell introduces Prime One cyber insurance product in the United States
item.description: Cowbell, an insurance technology company that provides adaptive cyber and specialty insurance solutions to small and medium-sized organisations globally, has announced the launch of Cowbell Prime One in the United States. The new product is a non-admitted cyber insurance solution created for organisations with significant digital risk exposure and annual revenues between $250 million and $1 billion. The US launch builds on Cowbell’s experience in international markets, where collaboration with global specialty re/insurance provider Chaucer has supported the development of cyber insurance solutions tailored to complex risk environments.
